zh.lifeSummary: Erenburga de Chateau du Loire是Mathilde的女兒，於1030年出生在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易。她和Gervais II de Château-du-Loir de Château-du-Loir至少有1個女兒。她去世於1100年5月10日在法國波特-哈倫斯哈倫特享年70歲，埋葬在法國安喬豐特福勞爾吉利阿巴易。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Erenburga de Chateau du Loire是Mathilde的女儿，于1030年出生在法国帕斯 德 拉 洛易。她和Gervais II de Château-du-Loir de Château-du-Loir至少有1个女儿。她去世于1100年5月10日在法国波特-哈倫斯哈倫特享年70岁，埋葬在法国安喬豐特福勞爾吉利阿巴易。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Chateau
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Thierry, Francois, Michel.
French (Château):
from Old French chastel (French château) ‘castle, fortified building’, a topographic name for someone who lived in or near a castle, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who worked in one.

variants.name: Chatel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Celine, Emile, Gilles, Jacques, Lucien.
French (also Châtel):
habitational name from (Le) Chatel or (Le) Châtel, names of several places in various parts of France, from Old French chastel ‘castle, fortified building’.

variants.name: Duchateau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Andree, Armand, Philippe.

French (Duchâteau); Walloon and Flemish (rarely Duchâteau): topographic name, with fused French preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, from Old French chastel (French château) ‘castle, fortified building’ (see Chateau ), or a habitational name denoting someone from Le Château, the name of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named with château.

nameMeanings.name: du
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Hong, Jian, Jing, Li, Ping, Yan, Yang, Yong, Bin, Chan, Cheng.  Vietnamese Thanh, Quang, Cuong, Dung, Hung, Binh, Minh, Duong, Hao, Hien, Liem, Lien.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 杜, meaning ‘birchleaf pear’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by descendants of Du Kang (杜康), an official noted as a winemaker during the reign of the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (ii) from the placename Du (杜), the name of a city (located in Shaanxi province) granted to Du Bo (杜伯, the Earl of Du), head of the state of Du (杜), who was eventually killed by King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782 BC ). After that, the state of Du (杜) was annexed and the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (iii) adopted as a surname by the Du Gu Hun (獨孤渾) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 都, meaning ‘capital’ in Chinese: (i) from the personal name Zi Du (子都), style name of Gong-Sun Yu, an official in the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound surname Gong Du (公都), which is sometimes traced back to Tian, a prince in the state of Chu who was granted the fief Du (都), or alternatively to Gong Du Zi (公都子), an official in the state of Qi.

variants.name: Doo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Jin Ho, Young, Jong, Ling, Mi Sook, Quong, Yi.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .
English (Surrey): probably a variant of   Dew  .

variants.name: Duh
meanings: Slovenian and Croatian: nickname from duh ‘spirit, ghost’.
Chinese: variant of   Du  .

variants.name: Do
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Minh, Thanh, Hien, Long, Dung, Anh, Tuan, Hoang, Hai, Quang, Son.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Chi, Sang, Dong, Chan, Han, Ho, Bai, Chien, Lai, Man.
Vietnamese (Đỗ): from the Chinese surname 杜, see Du 1.
Vietnamese (Đồ): from the Chinese surname 涂 (or 塗), see Tu 1.

variants.name: Dou
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 竇, meaning ‘hole’ in ancient Chinese: (i) adopted as a surname by Yue Zhu and Yue Long, two sons of Shao Kang, the sixth king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ). Yue Zhu and Yue Long are said to have adopted Dou (竇) (meaning ‘hole’) as a surname because their pregnant grandmother had escaped from a rebellion through a hole in the wall before giving birth to their father, Shao Kang, who later recaptured the throne. (ii) traced back to the Dou (竇) family from the Di ethnic group and some other families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 豆, denoting an ancient stemmed cup of bowl: (i) traced back to some inhabitants in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) adopted as the surname by the Chi Xiao Dou (赤小豆) and Dou Lu (豆盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 鈄, referring to a type of ancient vessel for drinking wine: said to be borne by the descendants of the Duke Kang of Qi (died 379  BC ), who was ousted from his throne in the state of Qi and sent into exile. It was said that he was forced to use the vessel Dou (鈄) to cook food during his exile, and some of his descendants inherited Dou as the surname to memorize this period.

variants.name: Yu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Ming, Yong, Young, Ping, Chi, Chun, Jae, Li, Sung, Jin, Yang.  Korean Chung, Chang, Byung, Chong, Min, Pyong, Myong, Hyong, Moon, Yiu, Inyoung, Shiu.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 余 (or rarely written as 餘), meaning ‘I’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the second element of the personal name You Yu (由余), the name of a prime minister in the state of Qin during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from Yu (余), the name of a small state during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iii) said to be borne by descendants of Xia Yu, the first king of the Xia dynasty (2070–1600  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 于 (meaning ‘go’ in ancient Chinese) or 於 (often simplified as 于 in Mainland China, meaning ‘depend on’ in ancient Chinese): (i) simplified form of the Chinese character 邘, the name of an ancient state (located in Qinyang in Henan province), which was granted to Yu Shu (邘叔), the third son of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ). After it was annexed by the state of Zheng during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ), the Chinese character 邘 (pronounced Yu) was simplified to (于) and adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Wan Niu Yu (萬忸于) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iii) adopted in place of the Chinese compound surname Chun-Yu (淳于), because the first element of the surname, Chun, happened to share the same pronunciation with the personal name of the emperor Li Chun, also known as Emperor Xianzong of Tang (778–820 AD ), which was taboo in ancient China. (iv) said to be traced back to Yu Ze (於則), who said to be the inventor of shoes, an official the legendary Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ). (v) from the second element of the placename Shang Yu (商於) in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ).

nameMeanings.name: Loire
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Loria  .

variants.name: Loria
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Vita, Aldo, Antonio, Filippo, Natale, Nunzie, Pietro, Sal.  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Luis, Alicia, Ana, Arturo, Basilio, Elvira, Estrella, Fermin, Francisco, Javier.
Italian: variant of   Lauria  , a habitational name from Lauria in Potenza.
Spanish (Seville): possibly of Italian origin (see 1 above). This surname is very rare in Spain; it is found mainly in Costa Rica, Mexico, and the Philippines.

en.lifeSummary: Erenburga de Chateau du Loire was born in 1030, in Pays de la Loire, France as the daughter of Mathilde. She had at least 1 daughter with Gervais II de Château-du-Loir de Château-du-Loir. She died on 10 May 1100, in Charente, Poitou-Charentes, France, at the age of 70, and was buried in Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Erenburga de Chateau du Loire est née en 1030, à Pays de la Loire, France, fille de Mathilde. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Gervais II de Château-du-Loir de Château-du-Loir. Elle est décédée le 10 mai 1100, à Charente, Poitou-Charentes, France, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterrée à Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, France.

de.lifeSummary: Erenburga de Chateau du Loire wurde 1030 in Pays de la Loire, Frankreich, als Tochter von Mathilde geboren. Sie hatte mit Gervais II de Château-du-Loir de Château-du-Loir mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 10. Mai 1100 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Charente, Poitou-Charentes, Frankreich und wurde in Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Erenburga de Chateau du Loire è nata il nel 1030 nel Pays de la Loire, Francia come figlia di Mathilde. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Gervais II de Château-du-Loir de Château-du-Loir. Morì il 10 maggio 1100, nel Charente, Poitou-Charentes, Francia, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolta a Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Erenburga de Chateau du Loireは1030年にフランスペイズ ド ラ ロイアーでMathildeの娘として生まれた。彼女はGervais II de Château-du-Loir de Château-du-Loirとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1100年5月10日にフランスポイトウ-チャーエンツチャーエントで70歳で死去し，フランスアンジョウフォンテvラウルト-ル'アッバイにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Erenburga de Chateau du Loire(은)는 1030년 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르에서 Mathilde의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Gervais II de Château-du-Loir de Château-du-Loir(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1100년 5월 10일 프랑스 포이토우-처츠 처트에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 앤조우 폰테브라트-'압베이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Erenburga de Chateau du Loire nasceu em 1030, em Pays de la Loire, França filha de Mathilde. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filha com Gervais II de Château-du-Loir de Château-du-Loir. Ela faleceu em 10 de maio de 1100, em Charente, Poitou-Charentes, França, com 70 anos, e foi sepultada em Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Erenburga de Chateau du Loire родилась в 1030 г. в местности пеис де ла лоир, Франция. Её мать звали Mathilde. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Gervais II de Château-du-Loir de Château-du-Loir. Она умерла 10 мая 1100 г. в местности чарент, поитоу-чарентесь, Франция в возрасте 70 лет и была похоронена в местности фонтевраулт-л'аббеи, анджоу, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Erenburga de Chateau du Loire nació en 1030, en Pays de la Loire, Francia como hija de Mathilde. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Gervais II de Château-du-Loir de Château-du-Loir. Murió el 10 de mayo de 1100, en Charente, Poitou-Charentes, Francia, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultada en Fontevrault-l'Abbaye, Anjou, Francia.
